requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'TTKV: Temporal-Tiered KV Cache for Long-Context LLM Inference' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: Models and datasets used in the evaluation have been acquired and prepared.
All evaluated LLMs are available for inference.
LLaMA-3.1-8B model weights are available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify Llama-3.1-8B weights are downloadable and loadable: from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM; AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B') succeeds and config.num_hidden_layers == 32.

sub_tasks.requirements: LLaMA-3.1-70B model weights are available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify Llama-3.1-70B weights are downloadable and loadable via HuggingFace transformers; config.num_hidden_layers == 80.

sub_tasks.requirements: Qwen2.5-32B model weights are available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify Qwen2.5-32B weights load via HuggingFace transformers AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B') and report ~32B parameters.

sub_tasks.requirements: DeepSeek-R1-14B (distilled variant) model weights are available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B weights load via HuggingFace transformers from 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B'; check model has ~14B params.

sub_tasks.requirements: Mistral-7B model weights are available for inference.

verification.spec: Verify Mistral-7B-v0.1 weights load: AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('mistralai/Mistral-7B-v0.1'); config.num_hidden_layers == 32.

sub_tasks.requirements: All benchmark datasets used in the paper have been prepared for use in evaluation.
The Qasper subset of LongBench has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load Qasper subset from LongBench via datasets.load_dataset('THUDM/LongBench', 'qasper'); assert split exists and has nonzero number of examples each containing 'input', 'context', and 'answers' fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: The MultiNews subset of LongBench has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load MultiNews subset via datasets.load_dataset('THUDM/LongBench', 'multi_news'); assert split is nonempty with expected fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: The RepoBench-P subset of LongBench has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load RepoBench-P subset via datasets.load_dataset('THUDM/LongBench', 'repobench-p'); assert nonempty with 'input'/'context'/'answers' fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: The GovReport subset of LongBench has been prepared for evaluation at the 16K context length.

verification.spec: Load GovReport subset via datasets.load_dataset('THUDM/LongBench', 'gov_report') and verify examples have average context length around 16K tokens (using model tokenizer).

sub_tasks.requirements: The Loong extended multi-document QA benchmark has been prepared for evaluation.

verification.spec: Load Loong benchmark (Wang et al. 2024) from its official repo or HuggingFace mirror; assert documents and multi-doc QA pairs are accessible.

sub_tasks.requirements: The RULER synthetic long-context benchmark has been prepared for evaluation across context lengths from 8K up to 128K tokens.

verification.spec: Generate RULER synthetic benchmark at lengths {8K,16K,32K,64K,128K} using the official RULER generation scripts; verify samples exist at each length with expected task subtypes (e.g., niah_single_1, vt, qa).

sub_tasks.requirements: The L-Eval benchmark has been prepared for evaluation at the 32K context length.

verification.spec: Load L-Eval dataset via datasets.load_dataset('L4NLP/LEval') and filter to examples with ~32K-token context; assert at least one task subset is loadable.

sub_tasks.requirements: The experimental infrastructure for TTKV has been set up.
Code is implemented in PyTorch with custom CUDA kernels, executable on both NVIDIA A100 and RTX 3090 GPUs.

verification.spec: Verify the codebase imports without error using `import torch` and runs a minimal TTKV forward pass on GPU. Confirm CUDA is available (torch.cuda.is_available()) and that custom CUDA kernels (if any) compile and load via torch.utils.cpp_extension or are usable. The reference H100 substitutes for the paper's A100/RTX3090.

sub_tasks.requirements: Latency is measured on the GPU using CUDA events with sufficient warm-up steps, and p95 per-token latency is reported.

verification.spec: Inspect the latency measurement utility: confirm it uses torch.cuda.Event(enable_timing=True) start/end pairs around per-token decode steps, runs >=N warmup tokens before measurement, and computes the 95th percentile across measured per-token latencies (e.g., numpy.percentile(latencies, 95)).

sub_tasks.requirements: Memory usage is monitored using NVML counters during inference.

verification.spec: Inspect/run the memory monitoring code: confirm it calls pynvml (nvmlDeviceGetMemoryInfo) or equivalent NVML APIs to read used/free GPU memory rather than torch.cuda.memory_allocated. Run during a short decode to confirm it returns sensible nonzero readings.

sub_tasks.requirements: Host-to-GPU KV traffic is measured by aggregating host-to-GPU memory reads triggered by KV cache fetches during inference.

verification.spec: Instrument TTKV slow->fast tier transfers so each AsyncPrefetch records bytes_transferred. Run a short decode and verify the aggregated host-to-GPU traffic counter equals the sum of bytes for all transferred (compressed) blocks (within rounding) and is reported in GB.

sub_tasks.requirements: All experiments use a batch size of 8 unless otherwise specified.

verification.spec: Inspect benchmark configuration files / runner CLI defaults and confirm batch_size == 8 is the default for all latency/throughput/traffic experiments.

sub_tasks.requirements: Token throughput is measured in tokens/sec under each context length and batch size 8.

verification.spec: Run a short generation (e.g., 64 new tokens) with batch size 8 and a fixed context length; the throughput utility should report tokens/sec computed as (batch * new_tokens) / total_decode_time_seconds. Verify the returned unit is tokens/sec and the value is positive and stable across two runs.

sub_tasks.requirements: ROUGE-L computation has been implemented for summarization tasks (MultiNews, GovReport).

verification.spec: Call the project's ROUGE-L scorer on a small (prediction, reference) pair pulled from MultiNews and compare against rouge_score.rouge_scorer.RougeScorer(['rougeL']) reference; assert |delta| < 0.01.

sub_tasks.requirements: F1 score computation has been implemented for the Qasper extractive QA evaluation.

verification.spec: Call the project's Qasper F1 scorer on sample (prediction, gold_answers) pairs and compare against the official Qasper token-level F1 implementation (qasper-led-baseline / allenai). Assert agreement within 0.01 on at least 3 samples including a yes/no, abstractive, and extractive case.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code measures memory usage, latency, and accuracy under identical context lengths across all baselines and TTKV to ensure fair comparison.

verification.spec: Inspect the evaluation harness code to confirm that for each (model, dataset, context length) configuration, all baselines and TTKV are invoked with identical context length, identical batch size (8), identical input prompts, and that memory/latency/accuracy are measured with the same instrumentation (CUDA events for latency, NVML for memory, identical scoring function for accuracy). Confirm via code inspection that no method-specific shortcuts (e.g., different prompt truncation) are applied.

sub_tasks.requirements: The TTKV method (Sections 4.1, 4.2, 4.3) has been implemented faithfully.
The Tier Layout (Section 4.1) has been implemented as a tiered memory architecture spanning GPU HBM and host DRAM.
The fast-tier resides in GPU HBM and stores recent KV states in full FP16 precision.

verification.spec: Inspect the TTKV cache implementation: verify that the fast-tier storage object is allocated on a CUDA device (tensor.device.type == 'cuda') and that its dtype is torch.float16 (FP16). Confirm via code inspection or runtime introspection that recent KV entries are appended to this tensor without quantization.

sub_tasks.requirements: The slow-tier resides in host DRAM and stores older KV states in compressed form.

verification.spec: Inspect the slow-tier storage object: verify it resides on host memory (tensor.device.type == 'cpu' or pinned host buffer) and that stored KV entries are compressed (quantized integer dtype such as uint8/int8 rather than fp16/fp32). Confirm via code inspection that evicted blocks are placed into this DRAM-resident structure.

sub_tasks.requirements: The number of tokens L_fast retained in the fast-tier is dynamically defined such that M_fast = L_fast * d_kv * b_FP16 does not exceed the allocated HBM capacity C_HBM.

verification.spec: Instantiate the TTKV cache with varying C_HBM budgets (e.g., 1GB, 2GB, 4GB) and a fixed d_kv. Programmatically assert that the computed L_fast satisfies L_fast * d_kv * 2 (bytes for FP16) <= C_HBM. Also verify that increasing C_HBM monotonically increases L_fast.

sub_tasks.requirements: The fast-tier holds the L_fast most recent tokens in FP16, prioritizing the most frequently accessed data in the fastest memory.

verification.spec: Construct a TTKV cache, append N > L_fast tokens, and assert that (a) the fast-tier contains exactly the most recent L_fast tokens in FP16 dtype (torch.float16), and (b) older tokens have been evicted to the slow-tier. Verify dtype via tensor.dtype == torch.float16 and verify token identity via stored position ids.

sub_tasks.requirements: Fast-tier entry selection considers recency and role importance (e.g., high attention scores) when partitioning K and V into K_fast/V_fast and K_slow/V_slow.

verification.spec: Inspect the fast-tier admission/eviction policy: confirm via code reading and a unit test that selection of K_fast/V_fast vs K_slow/V_slow uses both (a) recency (token position relative to current step) and (b) role importance derived from attention scores. The test should construct a scenario where a non-recent token has unusually high attention score and verify it is retained in the fast tier.

sub_tasks.requirements: The slow-tier KV cache is partitioned into fixed-size blocks of B_blk tokens for efficient memory management and cross-tier movement.

verification.spec: Populate the slow-tier with N tokens where N is a multiple of the configured B_blk. Assert that the slow-tier exposes ceil(N/B_blk) blocks, each containing exactly B_blk token slots (last block may be partial). Verify via cache.slow_tier.blocks attribute or equivalent API.

sub_tasks.requirements: Each slow-tier block B_j contains compressed representations of B_blk key and value vectors.

verification.spec: For each slow-tier block, verify that it stores both compressed keys and compressed values for exactly B_blk positions. Inspect block fields (e.g., block.k_quant, block.v_quant) and assert their size corresponds to B_blk tokens at the configured (8-bit key, 4-bit value) precision.

sub_tasks.requirements: A lightweight index idx maps slow-tier token positions to block identifiers, such that for i in T_slow(t), idx(i) returns the specific block B_j containing that token.

verification.spec: Populate slow-tier with known token positions. For several positions i in the slow-tier, call cache.idx(i) (or equivalent) and assert it returns the block B_j whose token range covers i. Negative test: positions in the fast-tier should not resolve to a slow-tier block.

sub_tasks.requirements: Newly generated tokens are first appended to the fast-tier in FP16 precision.

verification.spec: Run a few decode steps with a TTKV cache initialized below capacity. After each append, assert that the newly added K/V entries reside in the fast-tier and have dtype torch.float16. Verify by reading back fast-tier tensors at the most-recent position.

sub_tasks.requirements: A FIFO eviction policy is applied at the fast-tier: when the fast-tier reaches capacity C_fast, the oldest block of B_blk tokens is evicted, compressed via differential quantization, and appended to the slow-tier.

verification.spec: Drive the TTKV cache by appending tokens until the fast-tier exceeds C_fast. Assert that (a) the oldest B_blk tokens are removed from the fast-tier first (FIFO order), (b) those tokens are passed through the differential quantization function (8-bit keys, 4-bit values), and (c) the resulting compressed block is appended to the slow-tier storage. Inspect tier states before/after eviction.

sub_tasks.requirements: Tier Content (Section 4.2) is implemented as differential quantization in the slow-tier.
In the slow-tier, keys are quantized to 8-bit precision to preserve attention-score accuracy.

verification.spec: Inspect the differential quantization config / call sites for the slow-tier and assert that keys are quantized with bitwidth == 8. Verify by reading the configured key_bits hyperparameter and by checking that quantized key storage uses an 8-bit integer dtype (e.g., uint8/int8) or that the packed representation corresponds to 8 bits/element.

sub_tasks.requirements: In the slow-tier, values are quantized to 4-bit precision to maximize storage and transfer-volume reductions.

verification.spec: Inspect the differential quantization config / call sites for the slow-tier and assert that values are quantized with bitwidth == 4. Verify the configured value_bits hyperparameter equals 4 and that the packed value storage uses 4-bit packing (e.g., two values per uint8).

sub_tasks.requirements: A differential quantization operator Q is implemented such that Q applies Q_8-bit to key vectors and Q_4-bit to value vectors in the slow-tier.

verification.spec: Construct synthetic key and value tensors of known range. Invoke the differential quantization operator Q and assert: (1) keys round-trip with quantization error consistent with 8-bit precision (e.g., per-channel max error <= range/127 within tolerance), and (2) values round-trip with quantization error consistent with 4-bit precision (max error <= range/7 within tolerance). Verify keys and values dispatch to different quantizers via code path inspection.

sub_tasks.requirements: A corresponding decompression routine reconstructs approximate K_hat_j and V_hat_j from compressed slow-tier blocks B_j prior to attention computation.

verification.spec: Create a slow-tier block by quantizing a known FP16 K/V tensor pair via the differential quantizer, then call the decompression routine to produce K_hat_j, V_hat_j. Assert: (a) output dtype is FP16, (b) output shape equals original, (c) reconstruction error ||K - K_hat||/||K|| is below a small threshold (e.g., 0.05 for 8-bit keys) and ||V - V_hat||/||V|| is below ~0.15 for 4-bit values.

sub_tasks.requirements: Tier Interaction (Section 4.3) is implemented as the TTKV Streaming Attention algorithm (Algorithm 1) overlapping computation and communication.
At each decoding step t, attention is first computed over the fast-tier (K_fast, V_fast) using the query q_t (Algorithm 1, Line 1).

verification.spec: Run TTKV streaming attention for a single decoding step with a small q_t and populated K_fast/V_fast plus empty slow-tier. Trace/instrument execution to confirm that the first attention computation uses K_fast/V_fast as inputs (matching Algorithm 1 Line 1). Verify by hooking the Attn() call and asserting its first invocation arguments equal (q_t, K_fast, V_fast).

sub_tasks.requirements: A scoring function phi(q_t, B_j) is computed for each slow-tier block B_j to identify the most relevant blocks (Algorithm 1, Lines 2-5).

verification.spec: Invoke the streaming-attention routine with a synthetic query q_t and a slow-tier populated with M blocks. Verify (via instrumentation or by patching phi) that phi(q_t, B_j) is called exactly once per slow-tier block, that the returned scores feed into a TopK selection, and that J_scores has length M.

sub_tasks.requirements: Top-k slow-tier blocks are selected based on their phi scores (Algorithm 1, Line 6), implementing the sparse-attention nature of streaming attention.

verification.spec: Populate the slow-tier with N>k blocks and run one streaming attention step. Hook the top-k selection routine and assert that (a) phi(q_t, B_j) is computed for every slow-tier block, (b) exactly k block indices with the highest phi scores are returned, and (c) only those k blocks are subsequently decompressed and attended over.

sub_tasks.requirements: Non-blocking asynchronous prefetch from host DRAM to GPU HBM is issued for the selected top-k blocks (Algorithm 1, Line 8).

verification.spec: Instrument the cross-tier transfer routine (e.g., torch.Tensor.to(device, non_blocking=True) or cudaMemcpyAsync). For each top-k slow-tier block selected during a decode step, assert the transfer call is non-blocking (non_blocking=True is passed, or async API is used) and that the host buffer is pinned. Confirm that prefetch is issued for all k selected blocks.

sub_tasks.requirements: Prefetched blocks are decompressed using the differential 8b/4b quantization scheme prior to attention (Algorithm 1, Line 9).

verification.spec: Patch the decompression entry point to record the bit-widths used. Run a streaming-attention decode step that fetches at least one slow-tier block, then assert that the decompression was invoked with 8-bit dequantization for keys and 4-bit dequantization for values.

sub_tasks.requirements: PCIe transfers of slow-tier blocks are pipelined and overlap with attention computation on data already in the fast-tier, hiding the cross-tier transfer latency.

verification.spec: Run a 64K-context decode step with TTKV and capture a CUDA timeline (via torch.profiler or nsys). Assert that PCIe H2D memcpy events overlap in time with kernel execution events (attention kernels on fast-tier data). Quantitatively, assert overlap ratio > 50% of total transfer time. Compare against a no-pipeline variant to confirm the streamed version reduces wall-clock latency by approximately 3x as claimed.

sub_tasks.requirements: When len(K_fast) >= L_fast, the oldest fast-tier block B_evict is compressed and appended to the slow-tier and removed from the fast-tier (Algorithm 1, Lines 12-16).

verification.spec: Configure L_fast = N * B_blk for small N. Append tokens past capacity step-by-step. After the first overflow, assert: (a) one block of B_blk oldest tokens has been removed from the fast tier, (b) a corresponding new compressed block now exists in the slow tier, (c) the slow-tier block index includes the evicted positions, and (d) fast-tier length == L_fast.

sub_tasks.requirements: Cross-tier transfers are issued at block granularity (block-wise streaming) rather than as bulk monolithic transfers.

verification.spec: Instrument the host-to-GPU transfer call site (e.g., the cudaMemcpyAsync wrapper or torch.Tensor.to() invocations on slow-tier tensors). Run a decode step requiring K slow-tier blocks and assert that at least K distinct transfer operations were issued, each carrying at most one block's worth of bytes, rather than a single transfer covering all blocks.

sub_tasks.requirements: Default TTKV hyperparameters are configured as specified in the paper: 8-bit keys, 4-bit values, and a slow-tier block size of 128 tokens.

verification.spec: Load the default TTKV configuration object (e.g., the default constructor or YAML/JSON config) and assert: key_bits == 8, value_bits == 4, slow_tier_block_size == 128.

sub_tasks.requirements: All baseline KV-cache management methods used for comparison have been implemented.
The FP16 dense-prefix KV cache baseline (full-precision KV cache with no compression or offloading) is implemented and used as the reference baseline.

verification.spec: Instantiate the FP16 baseline cache and run a short decode loop. Assert: (a) all K/V tensors have dtype torch.float16, (b) no compression, quantization, or DRAM offload code paths are executed (verify via patched stubs that quantize/offload functions are not called), and (c) decode output matches a reference HuggingFace decode within FP16 numerical tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: The KIVI baseline is implemented with 2-bit asymmetric KV quantization.

verification.spec: Import the KIVI baseline module and assert: (a) it is registered as a baseline in the evaluation harness, (b) its KV quantization bitwidth config is 2 for both K and V, (c) it uses asymmetric quantization as described in Liu et al. 2024b. Verify by inspecting the constructor / config defaults.

sub_tasks.requirements: The KVQuant baseline is implemented with 3-bit KV cache quantization.

verification.spec: Import the KVQuant baseline module and assert it is registered as a baseline with a 3-bit KV cache quantization config. Verify the bitwidth setting equals 3 via constructor/config inspection.

sub_tasks.requirements: The DiffKV baseline (differentiated memory management with parallel KV compaction) is implemented.

verification.spec: Import the DiffKV baseline module and assert it is registered as a callable baseline in the evaluation harness. Confirm it implements differentiated memory management with parallel KV compaction by inspecting its public API (e.g., has methods/configs for differential precision and compaction).

sub_tasks.requirements: The ShadowKV baseline (KV cache in shadows for high-throughput long-context inference) is implemented.

verification.spec: Import the ShadowKV baseline module and assert it is registered as a callable baseline in the evaluation harness. Confirm presence of the offloading / shadow-cache abstractions described in Sun et al. 2025 via API inspection.

sub_tasks.requirements: Naive KV-placement baselines used in Figure 1 (all-in-GPU HBM, all-in-DRAM, and a uniform 50/50 split) are implemented for the design-principle ablation on Llama-3.1-8B.

verification.spec: Verify three additional KV-placement modes exist and produce expected residency: (1) all-in-GPU: all K/V tensors live on cuda device and no host tensors are allocated; (2) all-in-DRAM: all K/V tensors live on cpu device with PCIe transfers per access; (3) uniform 50/50 split: half of tokens (by position) reside on GPU, half on CPU. Assert these residency properties on a small populated cache.

sub_tasks.requirements: The design-principle motivating experiments from Section 3 (Figures 1-3) have been reproduced.
The all-GPU baseline produces OOM at sufficiently long contexts in the Figure 1 experiment, confirming HBM capacity limits.

verification.spec: Run Llama-3.1-8B inference with the all-GPU (HBM-only) KV cache placement at increasing context lengths up to 2^7 K (128K) tokens with batch size 8 on a single H100. Confirm that at some context length within the scanned range the run raises an out-of-memory error (CUDA OOM) or fails to allocate the KV cache, demonstrating HBM capacity limits.

sub_tasks.requirements: Figure 2 is reproduced by collecting per-element magnitude distributions of keys and values from Llama-3.1-8B and showing that keys exhibit greater dynamic range and variance than values.

verification.spec: Run a forward pass of Llama-3.1-8B on a sample input and collect the per-element magnitudes (|x|) of the key and value tensors across all (or a representative subset of) attention layers. Plot/compute the magnitude distributions (e.g., histograms, std, max-min range) of keys vs values. Verify that keys have strictly greater variance and dynamic range (e.g., higher standard deviation and larger max magnitude) than values, qualitatively reproducing Figure 2.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduction of Figure 3 confirms the baseline leaves the GPU idle approximately 78% of the decode time.

verification.spec: Reproduce Figure 3(a): run a single decoding step of Llama-3.1-8B with 64K context and batch size 8 using the baseline (bulk-transfer, non-pipelined) KV offloading strategy. Profile with CUDA events/Nsight to measure the fraction of decode wall time during which the GPU is idle (no kernel executing) while waiting for PCIe transfers. Report idle fraction as a percentage and verify it is approximately 78%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduction of Figure 3 confirms that pipelining reduces effective transfer latency by approximately 3x relative to the bulk baseline.

verification.spec: From timelines for a 64K-context, batch=8 decode step on Llama-3.1-8B, compute effective transfer latency (wall-clock time the GPU is blocked on / waiting for PCIe data) for both baseline bulk transfer and pipelined transfer. Report the ratio baseline_transfer_latency / pipeline_transfer_latency. Confirm value is approximately 3x.

sub_tasks.requirements: All evaluation runs reported in the main results (Tables 1-5, Figures 5-6) have been executed.
On L-Eval at 32K context, all five methods (FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, DiffKV, ShadowKV) and TTKV have been evaluated on Mistral-7B for p95 latency, Host-to-GPU traffic, and accuracy.

verification.spec: Execute the L-Eval (32K-context) evaluation on Mistral-7B for all six configurations (FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, DiffKV, ShadowKV, TTKV) with batch size 8. Confirm the output report contains per-method values for p95 latency (ms), Host-to-GPU traffic (GB), and accuracy (%).

sub_tasks.requirements: On L-Eval at 32K context, all five methods and TTKV have been evaluated on Llama-3.1-8B for p95 latency, Host-to-GPU traffic, and accuracy.

verification.spec: Execute the L-Eval (32K-context) evaluation on Llama-3.1-8B for all six configurations (FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, DiffKV, ShadowKV, TTKV) with batch size 8. Confirm output report contains per-method values for p95 latency (ms), Host-to-GPU traffic (GB), and accuracy (%).

sub_tasks.requirements: On GovReport at 16K context, all five methods and TTKV have been evaluated on DeepSeek-R1-14B for p95 latency, Host-to-GPU traffic, and ROUGE-L under a 4 GB HBM KV cache budget.

verification.spec: Execute the GovReport (16K-context) evaluation on DeepSeek-R1-14B for all six configurations (FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, DiffKV, ShadowKV, TTKV) with batch size 8 and a 4 GB HBM KV cache budget. Confirm output report contains per-method values for p95 latency (ms), Host-to-GPU traffic (GB), and ROUGE-L, and that the HBM budget enforcement is logged.

sub_tasks.requirements: On GovReport at 16K context, all five methods and TTKV have been evaluated on Llama-3.1-8B for p95 latency, Host-to-GPU traffic, and ROUGE-L under a 4 GB HBM KV cache budget.

verification.spec: Run TTKV and all five baselines (FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, DiffKV, ShadowKV) on Llama-3.1-8B with GovReport samples truncated/padded to 16K context, batch=8, and a 4 GB HBM KV cache budget. Record p95 per-token latency (CUDA events), Host-to-GPU traffic (NVML counters aggregating host->GPU KV transfers), and ROUGE-L. Verify all 6 method x 3 metric cells are populated in the produced GovReport-16K table.

sub_tasks.requirements: Token throughput is measured for FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, ShadowKV, and TTKV on Llama-3.1-8B at context lengths 4K, 16K, 32K, 64K, and 128K with batch size 8.

verification.spec: Measure end-to-end token throughput (tokens/sec) for FP16, KIVI 2-bit, KVQuant 3-bit, ShadowKV, and TTKV on Llama-3.1-8B at context lengths 4K, 16K, 32K, 64K, 128K with batch size 8. Each cell is computed as total generated tokens / wall-clock generation time after warm-up. Verify a 5x5 throughput table is produced (OOM allowed where applicable).

sub_tasks.requirements: Both FP16 and KIVI-2bit baselines on Llama-3.1-8B at 64K and 128K context lengths are recorded as OOM in the throughput table.

verification.spec: From the produced throughput table (Table 3 reproduction), verify the entries for FP16 at 64K and 128K, and KIVI 2-bit at 64K and 128K. The paper records FP16=OOM at 64K and 128K. For KIVI 2-bit, the paper actually reports 58.7 and 29.4 (not OOM). Verify FP16 produces OOM under a fixed HBM budget while KIVI 2-bit completes.

sub_tasks.requirements: On 128K Qasper, all six methods (including TTKV) have been evaluated on Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2.5-32B, and DeepSeek-R1-14B for F1 and p95 latency.

verification.spec: Run all six methods on Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2.5-32B, and DeepSeek-R1-14B with Qasper samples at 128K context. Report F1 score and p95 per-token latency. Verify all 18 (model, method) cells are populated.

sub_tasks.requirements: On 128K RepoBench-P, all six methods have been evaluated on Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2.5-32B, and DeepSeek-R1-14B for score and p95 latency.

verification.spec: Run all six methods on Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2.5-32B, and DeepSeek-R1-14B with RepoBench-P samples at 128K context. Report Score and p95 per-token latency. Verify all 18 cells are populated.

sub_tasks.requirements: On 128K RULER, all six methods have been evaluated on Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2.5-32B, and DeepSeek-R1-14B for accuracy and p95 latency.

verification.spec: Run all six methods on Llama-3.1-70B, Qwen2.5-32B, and DeepSeek-R1-14B with RULER tasks at 128K context. Report accuracy (%) and p95 per-token latency. Verify all 18 (model, method) cells are populated.

sub_tasks.requirements: Host-to-GPU traffic and p95 decode latency curves on RULER for Llama-3.1-70B are produced at context lengths 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K, and 128K for FP16, KIVI, KVQuant, ShadowKV, DiffKV, and TTKV (Figure 5).

verification.spec: For Llama-3.1-70B on RULER, sweep context lengths in {8K, 16K, 32K, 64K, 128K} and measure Host->GPU traffic (GB, aggregated from NVML counters during inference) and p95 decode latency (ms) for FP16, KIVI, KVQuant, ShadowKV, DiffKV, and TTKV. verification.spec: Run TTKV ablation on RULER (Llama-3.1-8B, 64K, batch=8) with (a) differential quantization (8b keys, 4b values) and (b) uniform 8-bit quantization for both K and V. Measure p95 per-token decode latency. Compute reduction = (uniform - diff)/uniform. Assert |reduction - 0.24| / 0.24 <= 0.25.
